zh.Marblethorpe, Lincolnshire, England: 英國英格蘭林可爾史爾

zh.Mablethorpe, Lincolnshire, England: 英國英格蘭林可爾史爾馬波索普
zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Thomas Fitz William of Maplethorpe於1300年出生在英國越可爾艾姆利時，他的父親William FitzWilliam47歲，母親Isabel Maud Deincourt25歲。他於大約1325年與Elizabeth de Mablethorpe在英國英格蘭越可爾艾姆利結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1345年在英國英格蘭林可爾史爾馬波索普享年45歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Thomas Fitz William of Maplethorpe于1300年出生在英国越可爾艾姆利时，他的父亲William FitzWilliam47岁，母亲Isabel Maud Deincourt25岁。他于大约1325年与Elizabeth de Mablethorpe在英国英格兰越可爾艾姆利结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。他去世于1345年在英国英格兰林可爾史爾馬波索普享年45岁。

nameMeanings.name: Fitz
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (from Latin filius), used originally to distinguish a son from a father bearing the same personal name and then conventionalized as a hereditary surname, or short for a surname of which fiz ‘son’ was the first element, for example Fitzhenry and Fitzroy .
Altered form of English   Fitch  .
German: from a vernacular pet form of the personal name Vinzenz (see Vincent ) or Vitus (see Vito , compare Veit ). Compare also Fietz .

variants.name: Fitzgerald
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Eamon, Liam, Aidan, Bridgid, Caitlin, Donal, Fitz, Fitzgerald, Ciaran, Colum, Dermot.

Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Gerald (see Gerald ), ‘son of Gerald’. This is a very common Anglo-Norman name in Ireland, where it has been Gaelicized as Mac Gearailt. Irish bearers of the name claim descent from Gerald Fitzwalter de Windsor, constable of Pembroke Castle in the early 12th century, whose grandson Raymond (or Redmond) ‘le Gros’ FitzGerald (died after 1185) led the invasion of Ireland on behalf of Strongbow in 1170. A branch of the family held the title Knight of Glin from at least 1299–2011.

variants.name: Fitzwater
meanings: Irish: altered form of Fitzwalter, a patronymic from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Walter , meaning ‘son of Walter’.

History: This is the name of a family of Norman origin, descended from Godfrey, Count of Brionne (died c. 1010). Godfrey's grandson, Richard FitzGilbert, accompanied William the Conqueror and was made Lord of Clare. His great-grandson was Robert FitzWalter, one of the barons who guaranteed the Magna Carta in 1215.

variants.name: Fitzherbert
meanings: English and Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Herbert (see Herbert ), ‘son of Herbert’.

variants.name: Fitzhenry
meanings: Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Henry (see Henry ), ‘son of Henry’. This surname is common in Ireland (mainly Connacht), where it has been Gaelicized as Mac Éinrí. In at least some cases, it seems to have been adopted as a surname indicating the illegitimate offspring of a man called Henry.

variants.name: Fitzsimmons
meanings: Irish and English: variant of Fitzsimon, with post-medieval excrescent -s, from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + either of the personal names Simon or Sigmund (see Simon ), ‘son of Simon or Sigmund’. The name is also found in the Gaelicized form Mac Síomóin.

variants.name: Fitzgibbon
meanings: Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Gibbon, a Norman French pet form of Gilbert ; ‘son of Gibbon’. This name has long been a widespread and important surname in Ireland, and Gaelicized as Mac Giobúin. Sir Gilbert (Gibbon) FitzJohn, illegitimate son of John FitzGerald, 1st Baron Desmond (died 1261), was the ancestor of a line of Anglo-Norman aristocracy in Ireland, who acquired the surname Fitzgibbon with reference to him. Gilbert and his Fitzgibbon descendants were known as ‘the White Knights’ on account of their fair complexion.

variants.name: Fitzharris
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Aileen.

Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Harry ‘son of Harry’. MacLysaght describes this as the name of a distinguished County Wexford family, also known as Fitzhenry .

variants.name: Fitzmaurice
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Aidan, Ciaran, Colm.

Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Maurice (see Morris 1), ‘son of Maurice’. This surname is common in Ireland, where it has also been Gaelicized as Mac Muiris. The Irish family of Fitzmaurice share a common Norman ancestry with the Fitzgerald family. They can be traced to Thomas Fitzmaurice, whose brother Gerald was the ancestor of the Irish Fitzgerald dynasty. Their father was Maurice Fitzgerald, a Norman baron (died 1176).

variants.name: Fitzhugh
meanings: English (Northamptonshire): from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Hugh (see Hugh ), ‘son of Hugh’.

History: William Fitzhugh (1651–1701), from Bedford, England, emigrated to VA c. 1670 and established himself on the Potomac River in what was then Stafford County, VA, as a planter and exporter. He also practiced law, was a member of the Virginia House of Burgesses, and served in 1687 as lieutenant colonel of the county militia.

variants.name: Fitzroy
meanings: English: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (from Latin filius), used originally to distinguish a son from a father bearing the same personal name and then conventionalized as a hereditary surname, or short for a surname of which fiz ‘son’ was the first element, for example Fitzhenry and Fitzroy .
Altered form of English   Fitch  .
German: from a vernacular pet form of the personal name Vinzenz (see Vincent ) or Vitus (see Vito , compare Veit ). Compare also Fietz .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): from the Middle English personal name William, Willam, Willem, an Old Picard and Norman French form of ancient Germanic Willihelm, composed of the elements wil- ‘will, desire’ + helm- ‘helmet, protection’. This Norman personal name became widely used in England, Scotland and Wales after the Conquest, mainly no doubt in honor of the Conqueror himself. By the 13th century it had already become the most popular personal name in England. A Parisian form of the name Guillaume was also sometimes used (see Gillam ), which in medieval Wales was adopted as Gwilym (see Gwilliam ).
Americanized form (translation into English) of French   Guillaume  .

variants.name: Memoli
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Ciro, Francesco, Luciano, Natale, Nino.
Italian:
patronymic or plural form of a pet form of thepersonal name Guglielmo, Italian form of William .

variants.name: McElligott
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brigid, Brendan, Seamus.
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Uileagóid, a patronymic from a pet form of the Gaelic form of the personal name William .

variants.name: McWilliam
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scottish Iain, Morag.

Scottish and Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Uilleim, a patronymic from a Gaelicized form of William , also Anglicized as Williamson in Scotland. The name is thought to be mainly Scottish in Ulster, though there has been some confusion with McQuillan in County Down. In Connacht, McWilliam was a branch of Burke .

variants.name: Wilcock
meanings: English (Lancashire and Yorkshire): from the Middle English personal name Wilcoc, a pet form of William (composed of the elements Will 1 + the hypocoristic suffix -cok; see Cocke ).

variants.name: McWilliams
meanings: Irish (northern): Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Uilleim, a patronymic from a Gaelicized form of William , with excrescent English -s. In Ireland it sometimes represents a partial Gaelicization of Welsh Williams .

variants.name: Lemmo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Donato, Gino.
Italian (southern): from the personal name Lemmo, a pet form of Guglielmo, an equivalent of William . Compare Lembo .

variants.name: Wilk
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Mariusz, Stanislaw, Tadeusz, Zygmunt, Andrzej, Bogdan, Boguslaw, Casimir, Krzysztof, Stanislawa, Boleslaw, Darek.

Polish: from the vocabulary word wilk ‘wolf’, probably applied as a nickname for someone thought to resemble a wolf or connected with wolves in some way. Alternatively, from an Old Slavic personal name based on vlъkъ ‘wolf’.
English (Staffordshire): from Middle English Wilke, a pet form of William and probably a shortening of Wilkin . The usage was very likely Flemish in origin.

variants.name: Wilkin
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Wilkin, a pet form of William introduced to England and Scotland by Flemings and Picards after the Norman Conquest.
North German: variant of   Wilken  .

variants.name: Gillam
meanings: English: variant of   Gilliam  .
Welsh: Anglicized form of   Gwilliam  .

variants.name: Lembo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Angelo, Carmelo, Domenic, Pietro, Alfio, Amelio, Antonio, Biagio, Dante, Giovani, Giovanni.
Italian (southern): from a hypercorrected form of Lemmo, a pet form of Guglièlmo, an equivalent of William .

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Thomas Fitz William of Maplethorpe was born in 1300, in Emley, Yorkshire, England, his father, William FitzWilliam, was 47 and his mother, Isabel Maud Deincourt, was 25. He married Elizabeth de Mablethorpe about 1325, in Emley, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died in 1345, in Mablethorpe, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 45.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Thomas Fitz William of Maplethorpe est né en 1300, à Emley, Everwyck, Angleterre, son père, William FitzWilliam, avait 47 ans et sa mère, Isabel Maud Deincourt, avait 25 ans. Il a épousé Elizabeth de Mablethorpe environ 1325, à Emley, Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé en 1345, à Mablethorpe, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 45 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Thomas Fitz William of Maplethorpe 1300 in Emley, Yorkshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater William FitzWilliam 47 und seine Mutter Isabel Maud Deincourt 25. Er hat um 1325 in Emley, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom Elizabeth de Mablethorpe geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb 1345 im Alter von 45 Jahren in Mablethorpe, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Thomas Fitz William of Maplethorpe è nato il nel 1300 nel Emley, Yorkshire, England, il suo padre, William FitzWilliam, aveva 47 anni e il suo madre, Isabel Maud Deincourt, aveva 25 anni. Lui sposato Elizabeth de Mablethorpe circa 1325, nel Emley, Yorkshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì nel 1345, nel Mablethorpe, Lincolnshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 45 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Thomas Fitz William of Maplethorpeが1300年にイギリスヨークシュアイアエムリーで生まれた時，父William FitzWilliamは47歳，母Isabel Maud Deincourtは25歳であった。約1325年にイギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアエムリーでElizabeth de Mablethorpeと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼は1345年にイギリスイギリスリンコルンシュアイアマブレソルペで45歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Thomas Fitz William of Maplethorpe(이)가 1300년 영국 요르크실르 리에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William FitzWilliam(은)는 47세였고, 그의 어머니 Isabel Maud Deincourt(은)는 25세였다. 그는 대략 1325년 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 리에서 Elizabeth de Mablethorpe(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1345년 영국 잉글랜드 링코실르 마블레소에서 45세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Thomas Fitz William of Maplethorpe nasceu em 1300, em Emley, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, William FitzWilliam, tinha 47 anos e sua mãe, Isabel Maud Deincourt, tinha 25 anos. Ele casou-se com Elizabeth de Mablethorpe aproximadamente 1325, em Emley, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ele faleceu em 1345, em Mablethorpe, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 45 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Thomas Fitz William of Maplethorpe родился в 1300 г. в местности эмли, еркшир, Англия, его отцу по имени William FitzWilliam было 47 лет, а матери, которую звали Isabel Maud Deincourt, 25. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth de Mablethorpe около 1325 г. в местности эмли, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер в 1345 г. в местности маблетаурп, линколншир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 45 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir Thomas Fitz William of Maplethorpe nació en 1300, en Emley, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, su papá, William FitzWilliam, tenía 47 años y su mamá, Isabel Maud Deincourt, tenía 25 años. Se casó con Elizabeth de Mablethorpe aproximadamente 1325, en Emley, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió en 1345, en Mablethorpe, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 45 años.
